components.name: Separator
components.description: A thin 1px line that visually divides content into distinct groups. Supports horizontal (h-px w-full) and vertical (h-full w-px) orientations. Purely decorative by default.

examples.name: Orientations
examples.description: Horizontal (full-width 1px line) and vertical (1px × 24px inline divider).

props.name: decorative
props.description: When true, hides from screen readers (role='none'). Set false to expose role='separator'.

props.name: className
props.description: Extend sizing or color — e.g. className='my-md' or 'bg-primary/20'

designTokens.description: Line thickness — horizontal uses h-px w-full, vertical uses h-full w-px

bestPractices.do: Use Separator to divide distinct content groups — nav sections, form fieldsets, metadata rows.
bestPractices.dont: Add a Separator between every item in a list — it creates visual noise. Use gap or padding instead.

bestPractices.do: Leave decorative=true (default) when the separation is already communicated by layout or context.
bestPractices.dont: Rely on Separator alone for semantic content grouping — use headings, sections, or fieldsets.

keyboard.action: Separator is not interactive — no keyboard interaction

notes: Default (decorative=true): renders role='none' — invisible to screen readers.
Never place focusable content immediately adjacent to a separator without proper landmark or heading structure.

related.name: Card
related.desc: For bordered surface containers that group related content.

related.name: Accordion
related.desc: For collapsible content sections with built-in dividers.

related.name: Breadcrumb
related.desc: Uses vertical separators between navigation path segments.
